epics.title: Render compact Jazz chord symbols from stored plain-text chords

tasks.title: Define a reference-backed display mapping
acceptance_criteria: The display convention is based on SMuFL chord-symbol glyph categories and official MuseScore, LilyPond, and Dorico chord-symbol guidance.
Major seventh renders as △7, minor renders with −, diminished with °, half-diminished with ø, and augmented with +.
Chord identity and unaltered extension numbers remain on the baseline.
Alterations and modifiers such as b5, #5, b9, #9, sus4, and add9 render smaller and raised.
The mapping preserves stored chord strings and distinguishes slash-bass notes from suffix modifiers.
done_definition: The approved design artifact contains an explicit source-to-display mapping table expressed as builder constraints.
Ambiguous or unsupported suffixes remain legible instead of being silently discarded.

tasks.title: Apply the notation mapping to alphaTab chord SVG text
acceptance_criteria: Only alphaTab chord-name text nodes are transformed.
The transformed SVG remains readable and idempotent across alphaTab rerenders.
Root and slash-bass accidentals use ♭ and ♯ display glyphs.
Representative chords render as C△7, C−7, C7 plus raised ♭9, and E7 plus raised ♯9.
done_definition: TypeScript, lint, and production build checks pass.
No source song data or alphaTex output changes.

tasks.title: Verify mobile fit and musical readability
acceptance_criteria: Browser evidence is captured at 390px for 기억의 습작 before and after the approved implementation.
Previously detected negative horizontal gaps between adjacent chord symbols are eliminated.
The page has no new horizontal overflow and representative rows remain fully visible.
A chord containing a sharp alteration is verified on a representative song.
done_definition: Visual checks cover notation semantics, dense-row fit, redundant labels, and available-width behavior.
The AI-team run validates with approved design and code reviews.

assumptions: The requested convention is the compact Jazz-symbol family rather than text-heavy maj/min spellings.
The user's explicit baseline-versus-raised distinction takes precedence where scoring applications expose several configurable conventions.
Source chord strings remain plain-text input and are normalized only in the rendered SVG.
risks: Chord-symbol typography has multiple established presets rather than one universal layout; the chosen mapping must be explicit and consistent.
A broad suffix parser could misclassify malformed or uncommon source strings, so unknown tails must be preserved.
alphaTab regenerates SVG nodes asynchronously, requiring an idempotent post-render transformation.
